items.title: University leader says her sector is 'most prominent' for rising antisemitism

children.content: The chancellor of Western Sydney University says tertiary institutions have hidden behind freedom of expression to avoid taking action on hate speech.

articles.title: Sydney uni chief regrets handling of pro-Palestinian encampments

children.content: A royal commission hears how both the universities of Melbourne and Sydney have improved processes for students to make complaints in the wake of antisemitic experiences by staff and students, which escalated in 2024.

image.altText: Mark Scott wearing a suit and tie and glasses sitting at a desk looking forward

articles.title: Jewish student tells of her disgust at TikToks in Auschwitz car park

children.content: Paris Enten has told the antisemitism royal commission her experiences of Monash University, including during an overseas trip, caused her mental health to deteriorate.

image.altText: A woman with long brown hair in a checked blazer and dark top stares off camera.

gallery.altText: A birds-eye-view of Monash's Clayton campus, with rolling green hills in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A birds-eye-view of Monash's Clayton campus, with rolling green hills in the background.

items.title: Disclosure scheme could have prevented intimate partner abuse, woman says

children.content: A survivor of family violence wants victims to have access to information about a person's previous offences.

image.altText: A woman stands silhouetted in front of a window.

items.title: GP clinic network hit by major cyber breach

children.content: Partnered Health, which operates more than 50 general practice and skin cancer clinics across Australia, has identified more than a dozen clinics where it fears patient information was taken.

image.altText: A white front reception desk of a medical clinic, with a white business sign, and a TV and open door in one corner.

items.title: Moira Deeming makes last-ditch plea to Liberal Party to retain candidacy

children.content: Victorian MP Moira Deeming faces being disendorsed as a Liberal candidate for refusing to apologise to former party leader Matthew Guy, after accusing him of assaulting her at a community event in May.

gallery.altText: Moira Deeming walking with a folder in her hand.

thumbnail.altText: Moira Deeming walking with a folder in her hand.

items.title: Disgraced footballer Nicky Winmar removed from AFL Hall of Fame

children.content: Winmar was found guilty earlier this month of three counts of unlawful assault and common law assault.

image.altText: Nicky wearing a grey coat and sunglasses.

items.title: Primary teacher played kissing game with student, jury hears

children.content: Former Gippsland teacher stands trial over continued sexual abuse of primary school student in the 90s

image.altText: A smiling, middle-aged woman with dyed hair takes a selfie in a bedroom.

items.title: Suppression order lifted on child abuse content producer in Victoria

children.content: A man covertly filmed women and children at a Mildura home and used AI to create explicit content.

gallery.altText: A man looks at the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A man looks at the camera.

items.title: Police investigate attempted sexual assault of Melbourne dog walkers

children.content: Police are investigating after two women reported that a man attempted to assault them before the dogs they were walking chased him away.

image.altText: Dogs chasing a man along a creek

items.title: Truck driver's sacking for $100k ice cream bungle was fair, finds commission

children.content: The Fair Work Commission rules a trucking company was within its rights to fire a driver after he set the temperature of his trailer full of ice cream more than 20 degrees too warm.

image.altText: A road train with the logo EHR in green.

items.title: Melbourne home sprayed with bullets for second time in a week

children.content: A shooter opened fire on Kealba home, in one of two drive-by shootings in Melbourne's north-west overnight.

image.altText: Bullet in street

items.title: The new political parties hoping to deceive 'useful idiots' in Victoria

children.content: New political parties are seeking to exploit Victoria's anachronistic electoral system in the upper house to harvest voters' preferences and funnel them wherever they please.

image.altText: A Victorian Electoral Commission white-coloured ballot box

items.title: Concerns over loophole allowing residents to shut down Victorian councils

children.content: Advocates say that unproven charges such as those against councillors at Hepburn Shire, triggering automatic stand-down laws, can bring any other council across the state to a shuddering halt.

items.title: Melbourne council bans e-bikes after six-year trial

children.content: Yarra Council says e-bike operator Lime refused to take responsibility for retrieving dumped bikes.

image.altText: Lime bikes close-up.

items.title: Two dead in truck crash at Heathcote, north of Melbourne

children.content: A car and truck collided about 9.45pm on the McIvor Highway at Heathcote.

image.altText: A crashed car and police vehicle.

items.title: Melbourne mother accused of showing IS propaganda to her children

children.content: Police allege a Melbourne woman facing terrorism charges showed Islamic State propaganda videos to her children about slaughtering "disbelievers".

gallery.altText: Child watches laptop with terrorist propaganda video featured

thumbnail.altText: Child watches laptop with terrorist propaganda video featured

avatar.alt: A man with dark skin in a suit looks at the camera

items.title: Sheep industry turns to natural remedy to beat blowflies

children.content: A biopesticide using tea-tree oil in nanoparticles has been developed to keep flies off sheep.

image.altText: Sheep on a farm

items.title: They waited six weeks for a sunrise that lasted just 24 minutes

children.content: Researchers spending winter at Australia's southernmost Antarctic research station gathered to celebrate the return of the Sun after weeks of darkness.

image.altText: Person taking a photo of a sunrise in Antarctica

items.title: Mystery remains over sudden departure of Mildura Hospital CEO

children.content: Mystery surrounds the departure of the chief executive of Mildura Base Public Hospital, who led the north-west Victorian hospital through its transition from private to public.

gallery.altText: Mildura Base Public Hospital CEO Terry Welch wears a white shirt and black suit.

thumbnail.altText: Mildura Base Public Hospital CEO Terry Welch wears a white shirt and black suit.

items.title: Man charged with murder over death in Victoria's east

children.content: A 43-year-old man has been charged with murder following the discovery of the body of a 58-year-old Morwell man in Victoria's east at the weekend.

image.altText: Police tape around a house at night

items.title: Minister demands answers from authority following corruption allegations

children.content: Victorian senior minister Melissa Horne says new allegations about union influence on state construction sites are "alarming", writing to the infrastructure authority to seek assurances they had acted appropriately.

image.altText: A woman with curly red hair, looking serious.

items.title: Police charge alleged organiser behind Melbourne hospitality arson attacks

children.content: Investigators say the 20-year-old man was a high-ranking member of The Commission, an organised crime group believed to have targeted the city's nightlife venues in recent months.

image.altText: A police officer arresting a man

items.title: Victorian teachers set to strike again following deadlocked negotiations

children.content: A fresh statewide strike by Victorian public school teachers will he held next week, after negotiations with the Victorian government reached an impasse.

image.altText: A large crowd of people in red shirts holding signs in the air walk down a city street lined with shops and trees.

items.title: Former Victorian Young Australian of the Year charged over imitation guns

children.content: Darcy McGauley-Bartlett has been arrested and charged with a raft of offences, including possessing imitation firearms and police uniforms.

gallery.altText: man standing in suit holding award, looking proud

thumbnail.altText: man standing in suit holding award, looking proud

avatar.alt: A headshot of Nate standing in front of a dimly lit building wearing a jacket.

items.title: Ravenhall and Fulham prison guards walk off job in fight for better pay

children.content: CPSU members at two Victorian prisons are striking today, asking for pay increases and parental leave.

image.altText: Image of a group of people standing outside, holding white and red union flags.

items.title: New Jewish students apprehensive about being on campus, commission hears

children.content: The president of a Jewish student body tells antisemitism royal commission that he had a relatively untroubled childhood until he went to university.

image.altText: A man with wavy black hair, clear rimmed glasses, a blue suit jacket, white shirt and striped tie stands near venetian blinds.

items.title: Proposal to cut blanket ban on climbing at Mount Arapiles Dyurrite

children.content: Climbing Victoria says proposed changes to the Mount Arapiles Dyurrite management plan will be a compromise between climbers and traditional owners built on "trust".

items.title: Violence against women under national spotlight after four deaths in four days

children.content: Experts are calling for more research and better funding for domestic violence prevention after four women and girls were killed in separate incidents last week.

image.altText: A composite showing photos of three women, and a fourth picture that shows the shadow outline of a girl

items.title: 'Frightening': Organised crime dominates the illicit tobacco market and it's now online

children.content: The process was simple and the service was speedy but the ease with which 7.30 acquired illicit tobacco products was concerning.

image.altText: Cigarettes on a table.

items.title: Businessman shuts down council by laying charges against councillors

children.content: Three more councillors at Hepburn Shire Council have been served with criminal charges by a private citizen, leaving only two councillors in office.

image.altText: A historic building in a main street.

items.title: Geelong set star Cat timeline, Hawk sent for surgery

children.content: Geelong are hopeful star forward Jeremy Cameron will return in time to play a part in their premiership push after suffering a brutal shoulder injury.

image.altText: A Geelong AFL star grimaces in pain as he comes off the ground with a medical staffer during a game.

items.title: That's life: Derryn Hinch's most memorable media moments

children.content: A man of colourful contradictions, Derryn Hinch was a larger-than-life fixture on Australian radio and television over five decades who died on Friday morning at the age of 82.

articles.title: Derryn Hinch, 'human headline' and broadcaster, dies aged 82

children.content: Hinch was known for his distinctive broadcast style, his time in the Senate, and his law-breaking crusades to expose sex offenders that earned him several stints in jail.

image.altText: Hinch, dressed in a red scarf, talks to reporters outside Parliament House in Canberra

articles.title: Derryn Hinch remembered as 'tough old bugger', 'crusader for justice'

children.content: Media identity Derryn Hinch is being remembered as a provocative but courageous journalist who was ahead of his time in his fight against institutional sex abuse.

image.altText: A man smiles while posing in front of a long coastline and blue sky.

gallery.altText: Derryn Hinch on the TV show 'Hinch'
Daryl Somers in a white tuxedo with dancer Patrice Smith in a ballgown and Derryn Hinch wearing a tango frilly shirt

thumbnail.altText: Derryn Hinch on the TV show 'Hinch'
Daryl Somers in a white tuxedo with dancer Patrice Smith in a ballgown and Derryn Hinch wearing a tango frilly shirt
